a: Returns `true` when the two connection errors being compared aren't the same.
Compares two connection states and returns [true](/documentation/swift/true) if they're different.
Compares two firmware statuses and returns [true](/documentation/swift/true) when they're different.

t: Connecting to Bluetooth Peripherals in Swift Playgrounds
a: Scan for peripherals and display them in your playground's live view.
Display and manage connections to Bluetooth peripherals in Swift Playgrounds.
A streamlined interface for connecting the central manager for the current playground page to nearby Bluetooth peripherals.
The errors you may encounter when connecting a peripheral to the central manager for the current playground page.
The error that occurs when a peripheral fails to connect to the central manager.
The error that occurs when a peripheral connection to the central manager fails.
The error that occurs when a peripheral rejects a connection to the central manager because there are too many others.
The error that occurs when the central manager is in a state that can’t make connections.
The error that occurs when a peripheral fails to connect to the central manager before the timeout period expires.
A delegate you use to respond to peripheral discovery and manage the lifecycle of connections.
A view that displays the connection status of a peripheral to the central manager for the current page and manages connections to other peripherals.
A value that holds information about a peripheral displayed in a connection view.
The states you use to indicate whether a peripheral’s firmware is current
The state that indicates that a peripheral needs a firmware update before it can operate correctly.
The state that indicates that a peripheral doesn’t need a firmware update.
The states that tell users how to proceed when connecting to and switching between peripherals in a playground page.
The currently connected peripheral has outdated firmware and can't be used.
The peripheral is in the process of connecting to a connection view’s central manager.
The connection to a peripheral has been lost.
A connection view’s central manager is scanning for nearby peripherals.
One or more peripherals have been discovered and can be selected.
The protocol you adopt to display an available peripheral in a playground page’s connection view.
A delegate you use to respond to user- and system-initiated interactions with the central manager’s connection view.
A value between 0 and 1.0 indicating a peripheral’s percent battery charge.
Tells the delegate that the central manager established a connection with a peripheral.
Tells the delegate that the central manager established a connection with a peripheral.
Tells the delegate that the central manager disconnected from a peripheral.
Tells the delegate that the central manager disconnected from a peripheral.
Tells the delegate that a peripheral has been discovered during scanning.
Tells the delegate that a peripheral has been discovered during scanning.
Tells the delegate that the central manager failed to establish a connection with a peripheral.
Tells the delegate that the central manager failed to establish a connection with a peripheral.
Tells the delegate that the central manager is about to attempt to establish a connection with a peripheral.
Tells the delegate that the central manager is about to attempt to establish a connection with a peripheral.
Tells the delegate that the state of the central manager has changed.
Tells the delegate that the state of the central manager has changed.
Attempts to connect the central manager to the specified peripheral.
Attempts to connect the central manager to a peripheral with the specified unique identifier.
Attempts to reconnect the central manager to the most recently connected peripheral.
An array of the peripherals currently connected to the central manager.
Tells the delegate that a peripheral has a firmware update available.
Tells the delegate that a new peripheral was discovered and can be displayed in the connection view.
Tells the delegate that a new peripheral was discovered and can be displayed in the connection view.
Tells the delegate that a new peripheral was discovered and can be displayed in the connection view.

Tells the delegate that a new peripheral was discovered and can be displayed in the connection view.
Tells the delegate that the connection view needs a title to display for its current state.
Tells the delegate that a connected peripheral is about to be disconnected.
Tells the delegate that a connected peripheral is about to be disconnected.
A data source that provides subviews for available peripherals.

A delegate that can receive messages from the central manager by adopting the [PlaygroundBluetoothCentralManagerDelegate](/documentation/playgroundbluetooth/playgroundbluetoothcentralmanagerdelegate) protocol.
A delegate you supply to make decisions about which peripherals are displayed in the view.
Disconnects the central manager from the specified, connected peripheral.
A value that indicates whether a peripheral needs a firmware update.
An image that represents a peripheral.
Creates a new, empty view that can be populated with items when peripherals are connected to the central manager.
Creates a connection view initialized from data you supply.
Creates a structure that holds information about a peripheral displayed in a connection view.
Creates a central manager that supports communicating with Bluetooth peripherals.
An image indicating that a peripheral may not function correctly.
A string containing the localized description of the error.
The name of a peripheral as it should appear in a connection view.
A Boolean value that determines whether the central manager is scanning for peripherals.
Displays the battery level for the specified peripheral.
Displays whether or not the specified peripheral’s firmware is up-to-date.
Displays an icon representing the specified peripheral.

Sets all of the information about the specified peripheral at once.
